## [03:01 - 06:02]

but the question is, will they actually pay you? If you promote for them and you actually make sales, are they actually going to pay you? And it might sound weird, but there have been plenty of situations that I know of, in fact, there's been somewhere I've been involved where you promote for someone, you make sales, and then they go out of business, or they just forget to pay you, or they don't track for you, or just all kinds of weirdness can happen. ## [09:04 - 12:05]

once you start promoting for launches. And I'll note one distinction. There's often a leaderboard in the pre-launch before the open cart. There's often a leaderboard just for leads, who sent the most leads in the pre-launch. And sometimes there's prizes for the leads. And then there's a leaderboard for the sales. Personally, for me, when I'm promoting for someone, I don't really care about how I do in that leads leaderboard because it's all about, you know, really it's about the sales. Who is this person? And then, and this is a way to start to get attention within your industry, build position. And as you move up the leaderboard, you get more attention. ## [12:05 - 13:42]

for the last day of the launch or something like that. This is something I invented. I call them their sprint bonuses. And it was, frankly, it was named after bicycle racing. I got the idea from the Tour de France. They have sprint bonuses. So there's like a race within the race. And often on the leaderboards, there'll be a leaderboard within the leaderboard, a race within the race. And those can be easier to win. So like if I do a leaderboard just for video two, number of opt-ins just for video two, that could be easier for you to get on than if it was for the overall leaderboard for sales or the overall leaderboard for leads. Or there could be some launches that just aren't getting as much support. And you can look in and say, wow, they're halfway through this launch and they don't have that many big names up there. I had to meet, maybe this is where I'm going to go and just go all out and try to do really well. And we'll talk about this later when we get to tactics. But there's ways you can really boost your position on these leaderboards by maybe putting together some amazing bonus package. So you can say, pick out one launch and say, this is the one launch where I want to do good. And I want to hit top 10 on this leaderboard. So I'm going to go all out. I'm going to put together a special promotion like we'll talk about in the next video. I want to put together a special bonus like we'll talk about in the next two videos and just go all out to try to get top 10. So I'll often do this. I'll go in and I want to be top five or I want to be number one or I want to be top three. And you'll pick your battles and you'll look for where you really want to perform to get people's attention, whether it's the person doing the launch or just attention within the industry.
